narration: Define deposit() and withdraw(double balance) functions.
In main(), declare double balance = 0 and int choice = 0.
Display 'Enter your choice' prompt with asterisk decorations.
Implement switch statement with cases 1-4 and default.
Wrap switch logic in do-while loop: while (choice != 4).
Return 0 from deposit() and withdraw() functions.
Compile and test program. Verify do-while loop works.
Implement show_balance function: display balance with 2 decimal places.
Include <iomanip> header and use std::setprecision(2) with std::fixed.
Implement deposit function: declare local variable amount, prompt user, read input.
In deposit function: if (amount > 0) return amount; else return 0.
Implement withdrawal function: declare local variable amount, prompt user, read input.
In withdrawal function: if (amount < 0) return 0; else return amount.
Add std::cin.clear() and std::cin.flush() after input to handle invalid characters.
